Paris Design Awards 2026
Wall of Fame
Paris Design Conference
Login
Submit here
Enter the competition now

If you believe design is about much more than making things look prettier – come with us on our journey.

DNA Paris Design Awards
Paris Design Awards 2026
Wall of Fame
Paris Design Conference
Login
Submit here
A FRESH DESIGN
2026 COMPETITION IS NOW OPEN !
Regular deadline : 8th May 2026
Enter here.
WHO IS IT FOR ?
You’re a designer with a recent piece of work that touches on at least one of these disciplines : architecture, interior, landscape, product, graphic.
Your work stands out, it makes an impact, and deserves international exposure.
You’re interested in getting feedback and connecting with the brightest design minds in the industry.
HOW DOES THAT WORK ?
Submit your work online, whether you’re from France, or any part of the world.
Results will be announced at the beginning of July and the Awards Ceremony will happened in Paris on October 2026.
If you win one of the 5 main categories, you’ll be a DNA Designer Of The Year !
If you win any sub-categories, you’ll be a DNA Winner and will get a DNA Award to crown you !
CATEGORIES
Architecture
Interior
Landscape
Product
Graphic
THE FEE ?
From 60 to 350€ per entry, DNA Paris Design Award is open to professionals and students.
This year you can submit your projects until May 8th 2026 (late deadline until May 22nd).
More info here.
AND THE BENEFITS !
By entering DNA Paris Design Award, you’ll get :
Access to the Summer Design Conference events in Paris
Invitation for 2 people to the Gala night in Paris for winners
Network, press coverage and fame !
More benefits here.

评审.text:
DNA Paris Design Awards
All submissions are evaluated by an expert jury of design luminaries.
The jury committee is comprised of designers, editors and creatives who are devoted to true excellence in design.
The evaluation of entries is based on a range of criteria that are constantly adapted to current standards.
Educator, Design & Brand Director
Simon Hamilton - UK

Simon Hamilton is an Educator and Design Director at YesColours. He is an Associate Lecturer and Super Practice Leader for the MA Interior Design Programme at the Royal College of Art, London.

Principal and Founder of Claudia Afshar Design Studio
Claudia Afshar - USA

Claudia, founder and principal of Claudia Afshar Design Studio, is a globally acclaimed interior designer renowned for creating refined and luxurious residential, commercial, and hospitality spaces. She has been working recently working on multi award winning textured surface Dekton ‘Ukiyo’ collection with Cosentino and rug collection with Mehraban.

AirLab & Formas.ai Founder
Carlos Banon - Spain

Carlos is an Architect specializing in Digital Processes and Advanced Manufacturing methods. He is Director and Co-founder of AIRLAB Singapore, Partner of Subarquitectura Architects, and Associate Professor of Architecture and Sustainable Design at the Singapore University of Technology and Design

Founder & Designer at Pilar Zeta Studio
Pilar Zeta - Argentina

Pilar is an Argentinian multimedia artist based in Mexico City, working at the intersection of philosophy, symbolism, mysticism, and postmodern architecture. She has done major exhibitions at Miami Art Week, MACO Art Week, Art D’Egypt. She got Grammy nomination for her collaboration as an art director for Coldplay and a Bronze Clio Award for Best Directing Video for Don’t Go Yet by Camila Cabello.

Designer, Creative Director, Founder of DESIGN FOR INDUSTRY INC.
Daisuke Kitagawa - Japan

After working for a Japanese consumer electronics manufacturer, Daisuke established DESIGN FOR INDUSTRY INC. in 2015. He has been involved in a variety of fields, both domestic and international, from furniture and daily necessities to traditional crafts, home appliances, robotics, advanced technology R&D, new material development, business development, and city branding .

Principal & Founder, Dubbeldam Architecture + Design
Heather Dubbeldam - Canada

Heather is a fourth-generation architect and the principal of Dubbeldam Architecture + Design, a Canadian studio recognized for its innovative and sustainable projects. Dubbeldam has received over 100 awards including the Professional Prix de Rome and the Royal Architectural Institute of Canada’s Architectural Practice Award – two of the highest honors for architectural practice in Canada

Founders & Designers at Waiting For Ideas
Jean-Baptiste Anotin - France

Waiting For Ideas is a Paris-based creative studio, founded in 2021 by Jean-Baptiste Anotin. We work on the interface between art and design, creating visually striking and thought-provoking pieces, installations and spaces.

Founders & Designers at 2LG Studio
Jordan Cluroe & Russell Whitehead - UK

Design Duo, 2LG Studio was founded by husbands Jordan and Russell in South East London.
Specialising in Residential Interior design and product design, they are known on the international design scene as tastemakers and award winning designers.

Architect, Curator & Researcher
Gabriela De Matos - Brazil

Gabriela is a Brazilian architect and urban planner, co-curator of the Brazil Pavilion at the 18th Venice Architecture Biennale (2023) and winner of the Golden Lion for Best National Representation. She served as co-president of the Institute of Architects of Brazil in São Paulo and is a professor at the Institute Escola da Cidade.

Furniture designer
Robert Sukrachand - Thailand

Robert Sukrachand is a New York City and Chiang Mai based furniture designer. In 2020, he was named as “one of the 20 names to know now in American design” by the editors of Sight Unseen.

Curator, Writer & Instagramer
Louisa Falkenberg - Sweden

With a background working in fashion, interior design and high-end real estate, Louisa Falkenberg has experience building homes, curating spaces and beautifying your way of living. Besides her popular Instagram account ”whylessisnotmore”, she is also a regular contributor to ELLE Sweden.

Founder of Dcontract & Records Collection
Rod Reynolds - France

Rod is the founder and creative director of Dcontract agency, well known for its work for brands such as Rick Owens, adidas, Lacoste, Etudes or Ruinart. Since 2020, Rod has developed Records Collection, dedicated to sourcing show space for fashion houses and international fashion show producers.

Founder & Architect at Tara Sakhi Studio
Tara Sakhi - Lebanon

Tara is a Lebanese-Polish architect and designer, exploring how unspoken gestures shape human connection. By adapting ancestral rituals to today’s world, she creates objects and environments that nurture shared experiences, shaping contemporary ways of being through materiality and craftsmanship. Her work embraces creative hybridization, evolving as it interacts with different cultures and daily practices, fostering cultural exchange.

Interior Designer & Founder of Maison Malapert
Michael Malapert - France

Michael is an interior architect of our times. Before founding his Design Studio, he joined the team of Philippe Starck and manaege large-scale projects in Shanghai, Bangkok, London and Marrakech.

Metal-based Designer / Furniture Designer / MihoMidu Founder
Amira Gama - South-Korea

Amira A Gama is a designer and artist who creates decorative yet functional works based on metal. Using mirrored glass as her primary material, she offers unique value through interaction with the user.

Founders & Designers of Various Associates
Q Lin & Dongzi Yang - China

Various Associates is an architecture and interior design practice working regionally and internationally on research-based design projects.VA was founded in 2017 as a team of designers from an international background with professional expertise in architecture, interior design, material design, graphic and interaction design.

Architect, Set designer and Digital artist
Riccardo Fornoni - Italy

Riccardo is an italian architect, set designer and digital artist. He started his career by founding Creamatelier in 2019, a playground for his ideas and designs. His works have been published in magazines like Design boom, Elle Decor, AD magazine, Ignant and more.

Founding partner at Natural Arquitectura
Ramiro Guerrero - Mexico

Ramiro has led projects in Mexico, France, the Dominican Republic, the United States, among others. His design approach is sculptural and artisanal in nature, primarily grounded in the use of color. His work has been featured on the covers of National Geographic and other international publications in Germany, the Netherlands, and Spain.

Architect & Founder of WGXYZ
Will Garner - UK

WGXYZ Studio harnesses the power of AI to redefine design in architecture and beyond, helping creators innovate faster, streamline their processes, and shape the future.

Partner & Landscape designer at Devillers & Associés
Sébastien Roussel - France

Sébastien Roussel is landscape designer d.p.l.g. at Devillers & Associés (D&A). He has designed numerous projects, mixing the living fabric with the urban fabric, combining natural and cultural heritage. He is also educator at Atelier 7 at the Ecole Nationale Supérieure du Paysage.

Zetafonts / TypeCampus / Kmzero Design
Debora Manetti - Italy

Debora is co-partner of the award-winning type foundry Zetafonts and Program Director for the TypeCampus project for typographic culture in the design community. She is also a co-partner and strategic designer of Kmzero, a visual identity studio based in Florence.

Architect & Founder of Mold Architects
Iliana Kerestetzi - Greece

Iliana founded MOLD Architects in 2011. The practice integrates research, architecture, landscape design, advanced computational design, parametric design, graphic design, interior design, construction management. The work of the practice has been widely promoted in group exhibitions and received numerous awards.

Architect & Founder of Dabbagh Architects
Sumaya Dabbagh - Saudi Arabia

Sumaya is a Saudi architect educated in the UK. She founded Dabbagh
Architects in 2008. Since, she has completed a number of significant projects such as Mleiha Archaeological Centre or Gargash Mosque that address intangible notions such as memory, identity and belonging.

Architect & product designer at Nicholas Préaud
Nicholas Préaud - France

Nicholas’ work converges into a vast and unclassifiable contemporary discipline. He connects several practices such as architecture, furniture or product design on a smaller scale, and research and development in construction, to more abstract and digital design works intended to exist only digitally.

Head of Computational Design at Cobe
Eliana Nigro - Italy

Eliana is an architect and head of computational design at Cobe. She has been involved in a large range of projects all over the world. She worked for 3XN architects for four years, after completing a post-graduation master in advanced design methods (MADI).

类别.text:
DNA Paris Design Awards
We are a platform to showcase the best and brightest with our annual design contest and award.
Our mission: to discover and celebrate fresh and creative designs from all around the world in
Architecture, Interior, Landscape, Product, and Graphic Design.
If you believe design is about much more than making things look prettier – check the categories that fit your works the best, enter your best designs and join our community !
Architecture Design
Interior Design
Landscape Design
Product Design
Graphic Design

DNA Paris Design Awards

LATE FINAL DEADLINE +10% / May 22nd 2026

You want more info than that?

Check our FAQs.
Or contact us here : support@dna.paris

FAQ.text:
DNA Paris Design Awards
Entry Process and Requirements
What are the steps for submitting an entry?

1. Online registration at http://dna.paris/submit
2. Sign up for a free membership profile
3. Once you’re a member, log in with your username and password, and you will be directed to the submissions page
4. Select category(s) and enter the details for the project
5. Fill in the required information and upload your submission
6. Proceed to payment
7. You will receive a payment confirmation email

Do I have to send a physical sample of my product?
What information do I need to provide to submit my work?
Do I need to be studying design in order to qualify for the “student” category and entry fees?
Do I need to pay the submission fee before I can submit my work?
What are the requirements for the supporting images and documents?
Can I cancel my submission and get a refund?
Can I make changes to my entry after I have paid?
What should I do if I have trouble uploading my images?
How will I know if you have received my entry?
How will I know if my submission is complete, and what do I do if it’s not?
What is the “Entry History” feature of my Member page and how do I use it?
Categories and Submission
Can I enter one design into multiple categories?

Yes, you may enter the same design into as many categories as you see fit. In fact, doing so may increase your chances of winning. However, there is an additional fee for each additional category you enter.

Is there a limit to the number of entries I can submit?
Can a collection of designs be submitted together, or should I submit each design separately?
Does my project need to be completed to be submitted?
Do I have to be a designer to participate?
Do I have to provide plans or can I just submit photographs?
Use of Images
How will my images be used?

Your images will only be used for purposes of promotion of the DNA Paris. Through our global reach, your image will be viewed with your credit all around the world. All participating designers thus far have been extremely happy with the manner in which their images have been used and promoted globally.
Copyright and all other rights remain with the designer. Any images used by DNA Paris shall carry the designer’s credit line. DNA Paris may use images in any media sponsor publications. All entrants understand that any image submitted to the competition may be used by DNA Paris for marketing and promotional purposes including in any medium such as exhibitions, print, and digital media directly related to the DNA Paris competition though there may not be monetary compensation
Neither DNA Paris affiliates or partners assume any responsibility for materials submitted in violation of competition rules or for those which violate copyright regulations. DNA Paris will investigate claims of copyright infringement to the best of its ability and will remove and disqualify submissions that are clearly demonstrated to violate copyright and/or competition policies. DNA Paris will act to maintain the integrity of the competition and its affiliated entities but is not responsible for any damages resulting from images submitted in violation of rules and regulations.

Will I be compensated for the use of my images?
Payment
Can I make changes to my entry after I have paid?

No, after payment has been made, entries cannot be altered. Please review your work carefully before finalizing payment.

How can I pay for the entry that I submitted?
Are all the fees in Euros?
Judging
Who are the Judges?

All submissions are evaluated by an expert jury of design luminaries. The jury committee is comprised of designers, editors and creatives who are devoted to true excellence in design.
For a complete list of the jury members : http://dna.paris/jury/

Can I Receive Judges Rating or Comments?
Will I be notified if I’ve won an award?
Do the category winners receive an award or certificate at the Winners Cocktail Event?
When can I start using the winner logo?
When can I start communicating news of my award?
Who should I contact if I have additional questions?

关于.text:
DNA Paris Design Awards
Who is behind DNA Paris?

Farmani Group, established in 1985, is the organizer of International Design Awards (IDA), Architecture MasterPrize (AMP), European Product Design Awards (EPDA), Prix de la Photographie in Paris (PX3), London International Creative Awards (LICC), International Photography Awards (IPA) and the Annual Lucie Awards for Photography, which has emerged as one of the world’s most prestigious photography awards.

In Between is a Paris-based collective of thinkers and makers that come together to create fresh things where you wouldn’t expect. From launching fashion brand « supermarket paris » from scratch, to building and designing a rooftop or producing various content and documentaries, these guys push the boundaries of what having a multi-disciplinary mindset means.

What is DNA Paris?

There are two main pillars to DNA Paris :
1. DNA Paris Design Awards is an accolade honoring the work of international architects and designers, who improve our daily lives through practical, beautiful and innovative design.
2. DNA Paris Summer Design Conference is a series of fun and insightful events designed for curious minds willing to learn, discuss and anticipate what’s next in design.

Registration

To register for the DNA Paris Design Award, create an account on the DNA Paris website and submit your work online.
For Entries: http://dna.paris/submit/login.php

Friends & partners
